label: Narrow-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with transverse MID-HALL + rear spur — full-width bands front-to-rear: great_room (entry), wet band (kitchen + common + passage), transverse hall, master + ensuite band, standard pair at the rear. Canonical-size parti with paired bedrooms instead of a full corridor. gr style.

adjacencies.note: kitchen fills the west of the wet band directly behind the great_room — open connection (gr style)
the hall's east passage rises past the wet band to mouth into the great_room's rear-east corner (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
kitchen (west) and common T&B (east) share the wet band's dividing wall — stack 1
common doors south into the transverse hall
master (west of its band) doors north into the transverse hall
ensuite sits center-east of master's band, vertically aligned with common across the hall — interior door from master
br2 (rear-west) doors onto the spur terminus — the hall's east spur runs past ensuite to the rear band
br3 (rear-east) doors onto the spur terminus beside br2
the standard pair shares the rear band side-by-side — wall only

soft_proximities.note: ensuite and common are separated only by the 1.0 m hall — keep them stacked so both baths ride one under-slab run despite the ds designation

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_front_back_split_baths_ds_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Depth is the budget here: 3.2 (great) + 2.1 (wet band) + 1.0 (hall) + 3.5 (master band) + 3.1 (rear pair) = ~12.9 m. The bands can shave to ~12.3 m total before rooms hit hard mins; below that, or below ~6.2 m width (rear pair 3.0+3.2), fall back to split_columns.

notes: 3BR narrow catalog topology #3 — FRONT_BACK split with transverse mid-hall
and rear spur. Natural floor band ~78-90 m²; designed against the canonical
test: 10.5 x 17.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 6.5 x 13.0 m shell (84.5 m²).
Regime: single-detached, no firewalls — this parti DEPENDS on it (see below).
Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom):
Rear:  [br2          | br3               ]
..     [master       | ensuite | SPUR    ]
..     [---- transverse HALL ----        ]
..     [kitchen      | common  | passage ]
Front: [great_room (entry, full width)   ]
Program: five full-width bands, no long corridor. Great_room fronts the
street; the wet band behind it packs kitchen (west, open to great) and
common T&B, with a 1.0 m passage on the east linking great_room to the
transverse hall. The hall serves common, master and the east spur, which
runs past the ensuite to the rear standard pair. Master sits mid-depth —
buffered from both the street and the kids' rooms.
Why no-firewall matters: master and ensuite occupy a MID-DEPTH band with no
front or rear wall — they vent through the 2.0 m side yards (legal courts
for a single-storey under PD 1096 Rule 7/8). In the firewall regime this
band would be landlocked and the parti dies.
Plumbing: kitchen+common share stack 1; ensuite is nominally ds but sits
directly across the 1.0 m hall from common (soft weight 25) — in practice
one under-slab run. Note the dirty kitchen lands behind br3, not the
kitchen — the service path runs along the east side yard.
Differentiator vs side_corridor (#2): bedrooms pair in bands instead of
stacking on a column, so the standard pair gets rear-yard windows and the
master gets a quiet mid position; the cost is the dog-leg circulation
(passage → hall → spur). Prefer this on briefs valuing rear-facing kids'
rooms; prefer #2 when the kitchen must own the service edge.
Carport policy (deferred): one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m later (lot +1.0 m);
mirror so the passage/spur side faces the carport.
